text: A Merchant Category Code (MCC) is a 4-digit ISO 18245 code the card network assigns to a merchant based on what it sells. It is the single biggest factor in whether a card reward applies, because most Indian card reward tiers and exclusions are defined by MCC (for example fuel = 5541, groceries = 5411, marketplaces = 5262). The same brand can settle under different MCCs, and a card's reward hinges on the MCC the issuer actually uses.

Cashback is credited in rupees (as statement credit or Amazon Pay balance) at a fixed value. Reward points accrue at a rate and are worth a variable rupee value on redemption (often around ₹0.25 per point, but it depends on the card and redemption path). RewardTrust shows points with an estimated rupee value, clearly labelled as an estimate.

A reward cap is the maximum reward you can earn in a tier per billing cycle or month. Once you hit it, extra spend in that tier earns nothing (or drops to the base rate). For example a card may cap 5% online cashback at ₹1,500 per statement cycle.

Most cards exclude certain categories from rewards entirely, defined by MCC. Common exclusions across Indian cards are fuel, rent, wallet loads, utilities, insurance, government payments, jewellery, and EMI. Spends in an excluded MCC earn zero (or only the base rate), even if the merchant looks like a normal purchase.

Most cards do not earn reward points or cashback on fuel (MCC 5541/5542). What they usually offer instead is a fuel surcharge waiver — the ~1% transaction surcharge is waived within limits — which is not the same as earning a reward.

Loading money into a prepaid wallet is treated as a quasi-cash / wallet MCC (e.g. 6540 or 6051) and is almost universally excluded from card rewards. Paying a merchant directly with the card usually earns more than routing through a wallet.

The Amazon Pay ICICI card earns 5% on Amazon.in for Amazon Prime members and 3% for non-Prime members. Membership therefore changes the reward, which is why RewardTrust asks whether you're a Prime member.

Cashback and points often post 1–2 billing cycles after the transaction, not instantly. If everything else checks out (right card, eligible MCC, within cap), a missing reward may simply not have posted yet.

RuPay credit cards can be linked to UPI, so you can scan a UPI QR and pay from your credit card. Reward eligibility still depends on the merchant's MCC and the card's UPI reward terms, which are often different from (and lower than) the card's regular online rates.

RewardTrust's confidence score reflects how reliable a reward prediction is: it's higher when the rule comes from official terms and was verified recently, and lower when the source is older or the merchant's MCC is uncertain. It is a prediction, not a guarantee — terms can change without notice.
